assertions.title: Create User Form Validates and Submits

steps.action: Login with admin credentials and navigate to /admin/users
steps.expected: Users list page renders with Add User button
steps.observed: Users list page rendered with 7 users, Add User button visible

steps.expected: Create User modal/dialog opens with required fields
steps.observed: Create User dialog opened with fields: Email, Display Name, Roles (checkboxes for ROLE_ADMIN, ROLE_ACCOUNTING, ROLE_FACTORY, ROLE_SALES, ROLE_DEALER), Companies (Mock Training Co checkbox), Cancel and Create User buttons

steps.expected: Validation errors appear for required fields
steps.observed: Validation errors displayed: 'Email is required' and 'Display name is required'. Dialog stayed open, form was not submitted.

steps.action: Fill form with valid data: email=uat-test-r3-grpb@example.com, display name=UAT Test Round3 GroupB, roles=ACCOUNTING checked, company=Mock Training Co checked
steps.expected: Form populated correctly, ready for submission
steps.observed: Form fields populated correctly with valid data

steps.expected: POST /api/v1/admin/users returns 201, user added to list
steps.observed: POST /api/v1/admin/users returned 500 Internal Server Error. Toast notification displayed: 'Failed to create user — Request failed with status code 500'. Dialog remained open with form data intact.

evidence.consoleErrors: POST /api/v1/admin/users returned 500 Internal Server Error. Also minor React key prop warning in MultiSelect component (non-blocking).

assertions.issues: Backend POST /api/v1/admin/users returns 500 for all payloads. This is a persistent backend bug present since Round 1. The frontend form, validation, and error handling all work correctly — the issue is entirely server-side. Frontend properly displays the error toast and preserves form state.

summary: Tested 1 assertion: VAL-ADMIN-004 FAILED. Frontend create user form works correctly: dialog opens, renders required fields (email, display name, roles, companies), validation fires on empty submit showing 'Email is required' and 'Display name is required'. However, the backend POST /api/v1/admin/users returns 500 Internal Server Error for all payloads — this is the same persistent backend bug from Round 1 and Round 2. The frontend is not at fault; the backend endpoint is broken.
